About

  • Technical Demo built using Quarto

  • All data used in this report is synthetic, and is only used demonstratively for future prototypes using live data

  • Everything in the Automated Outputs section is built using templates, and so is automatically generated, meaning some elements need to be tweaked.

Table Of Organisations

FTE By Running Costs in 2023 (All Orgs)

Automated Outputs

Summaries By Classification

Commercial


Stats for all Commercial organisations:

Organisations in Scope: 3

Total Staff in 2023: 6.56 K

Total Running Costs in 2023: £61.5 M

Total Investment Costs in 2023: £2.61 M

Total Funding in 2023: £36.7 M

Commercial Organisations: FTE By Running Costs

Comparing Commercial organisations against all other organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot

clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

clas_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = parent_company))+
  geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(classification == clas_highlight,
                             label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

# plotly::ggplotly(clas_plot)
clas_plot

Reveal code 
# option to download data
latest_orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
  download_this(output_name =
                            paste0(Sys.Date(), " - ", unique(latest_orgs_data$time), 
                                   " - ", clas_highlight, 
                                   " Directory Data"),
                            button_label = paste0("Download ",
                                                  clas_highlight,
                                                  " Directory"),
                            output_extension = ".xlsx",
                            button_type = "success",
                            has_icon = TRUE,
                            self_contained = TRUE,
                            icon = "fa fa-save")

Finance


Stats for all Finance organisations:

Organisations in Scope: 4

Total Staff in 2023: 8.28 K

Total Running Costs in 2023: £134.63 M

Total Investment Costs in 2023: £7.51 M

Total Funding in 2023: £95 M

Finance Organisations: FTE By Running Costs

Comparing Finance organisations against all other organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot

clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

clas_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = parent_company))+
  geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(classification == clas_highlight,
                             label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

# plotly::ggplotly(clas_plot)
clas_plot

Reveal code 
# option to download data
latest_orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
  download_this(output_name =
                            paste0(Sys.Date(), " - ", unique(latest_orgs_data$time), 
                                   " - ", clas_highlight, 
                                   " Directory Data"),
                            button_label = paste0("Download ",
                                                  clas_highlight,
                                                  " Directory"),
                            output_extension = ".xlsx",
                            button_type = "success",
                            has_icon = TRUE,
                            self_contained = TRUE,
                            icon = "fa fa-save")

Stats for Capcom:

Organisations in Scope: 1

Capcom Organisations: FTE By Running Costs

All Organisations Under Capcom

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot
# org_highlight <- organisation
# clas_highlight <- classification
parent_highlight <- parent_company

# plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = parent_company))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = "dodgerblue")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(parent_company == parent_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Reveal code 
# option to download data
latest_orgs_data %>% 
    filter( parent_company == parent_highlight) %>% 
  download_this(output_name =
                            paste0(Sys.Date(), " - ", unique(latest_orgs_data$time), 
                                   " - ", parent_highlight, 
                                   " Directory Data"),
                            button_label = paste0("Download ",
                                                  parent_highlight,
                                                  " Directory"),
                            output_extension = ".xlsx",
                            button_type = "success",
                            has_icon = TRUE,
                            self_contained = TRUE,
                            icon = "fa fa-save")

#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
#                                    classification ==
#                                        "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = parent_company))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = "dodgerblue")+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(parent_company == parent_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Reveal code 
# option to download data
latest_orgs_data %>% 
    filter( parent_company == parent_highlight) %>% 
  download_this(output_name =
                            paste0(Sys.Date(), " - ", unique(latest_orgs_data$time), 
                                   " - ", parent_highlight, 
                                   " Directory Data"),
                            button_label = paste0("Download ",
                                                  parent_highlight,
                                                  " Directory"),
                            output_extension = ".xlsx",
                            button_type = "success",
                            has_icon = TRUE,
                            self_contained = TRUE,
                            icon = "fa fa-save")

Summaries By Organisation

Abstergo Industries


Stats for Abstergo Industries:

Classification: Research and Development

CEO: Emma Lewis

Contact Number: 403-257-8913

Staff in 2023: 1.82 K

Running Costs in 2023: £48.91 M

Investment Costs in 2023: £113.73 M

Funding in 2023: £35.46 M

Abstergo Industries: FTE By Running Costs

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot
org_highlight <- organisation
clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Abstergo Industries: FTE Over Time

Compared Against Other Research and Development Organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

Reveal code 
    # geom_label(data = filter(latest_orgs_data,
    #                          organisation == org_highlight), 
    #            aes(label = organisation), 
    #            fill = plot_color_hightlight, 
    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

Reveal code 
    # geom_label(data = filter(latest_orgs_data,
    #                          organisation == org_highlight), 
    #            aes(label = organisation), 
    #            fill = plot_color_hightlight, 
    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

Aperture Science: FTE By Running Costs

Reveal code

# scatter plot

clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Crichton Davies: FTE Over Time

Compared Against Other Finance Organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

Lockhart Gardner and Canning


Stats for Lockhart Gardner and Canning:

Classification: Legal

CEO: Sophia Young

Contact Number: 987-654-3212

Staff in 2023: 0

Running Costs in 2023: £54.32 M

Investment Costs in 2023: £4.31 M

Funding in 2023: £48.79 M

Lockhart Gardner and Canning: FTE By Running Costs

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot
org_highlight <- organisation
clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Lockhart Gardner and Canning: FTE Over Time

Compared Against Other Legal Organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

Reveal code 
    # geom_label(data = filter(latest_orgs_data,
    #                          organisation == org_highlight), 
    #            aes(label = organisation), 
    #            fill = plot_color_hightlight, 
    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

Reveal code 
    # geom_label(data = filter(latest_orgs_data,
    #                          organisation == org_highlight), 
    #            aes(label = organisation), 
    #            fill = plot_color_hightlight, 
    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

# scatter plot

clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Wolfram and Hart: FTE Over Time

Compared Against Other Legal Organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)

Reveal code 
    # geom_label(data = filter(latest_orgs_data,
    #                          organisation == org_highlight), 
    #            aes(label = organisation), 
    #            fill = plot_color_hightlight, 
    #            color = "white",
    #            nudge_x = -1.4,
    #            nudge_y = -10)

Wolowitz and Koothrappali


Stats for Wolowitz and Koothrappali:

Classification: Legal

CEO: Jackson Baker

Contact Number: 512-346-7892

Staff in 2023: 0

Running Costs in 2023: £8.71 M

Investment Costs in 2023: £557.43 K

Funding in 2023: £6.73 M

Wolowitz and Koothrappali: FTE By Running Costs

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# cant give chunks names as they are duplicated by the code
# scatter plot
org_highlight <- organisation
clas_highlight <- classification

plot_color_hightlight <- case_when(.default = "green1", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Research and Development" ~ "dodgerblue", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Medical" ~ "firebrick", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Finance" ~ "seagreen", 
                                   classification ==
                                       "Commercial" ~ "darkorange",  
                                   classification ==
                                       "Legal" ~ "darkviolet")

ggplot2::ggplot(latest_orgs_data,
                aes(x = fte, y = running_costs, fill = classification))+
    geom_jitter(size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)+ 
  scale_x_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
  scale_y_continuous(labels =
                         label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()))+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation,
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = TRUE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    ylab("Running Costs (£)")

Wolowitz and Koothrappali: FTE Over Time

Compared Against Other Legal Organisations

Reveal code 
#|: warning: FALSE
# time series

orgs_data %>% 
    filter(classification == clas_highlight) %>% 
    ggplot2::ggplot(aes(x = time, y = fte))+
    geom_line(aes(group = organisation), color = plot_color_hightlight, linewidth = 1.4)+
    gghighlight::gghighlight(organisation == org_highlight, label_key = organisation, 
                             use_group_by = FALSE, use_direct_label = FALSE)+
    theme_minimal()+
    xlab("Year (March 31st)") +
    ylab("Staff Employed (FTE)") +
    geom_point(data = latest_orgs_data %>% 
                 filter(classification == clas_highlight, 
                        organisation == org_highlight),
               aes(x = time, y = fte, fill = classification), 
               size = 4.5, shape = 21, fill = plot_color_hightlight)
